fields.label: Environment ID
fields.description: Get environment ID from your instance URL: [environmentId].live.dynatrace.com

overrides.description: Enter the URL for your dynatrace instance: myenv.live.dynatrace.com

fields.label: Dynatrace API Token
fields.description: The API token for the Dynatrace API. This is required for Older api endpoints on Dynatrace like Metrics, Problems, Logs, etc.

fields.label: Dynatrace Platform Token
fields.description: The Platform token for the Dynatrace Platform API. This is required for Newer api endpoints on Dynatrace like Grail

fields.label: Timeout
fields.description: The timeout for the HTTP client in seconds. Default is 30 seconds.

fields.label: With CA Cert
fields.description: Needed for verifying self-signed TLS Certificates

fields.label: CA Cert
fields.description: TLS/SSL Certs are encrypted and stored in the Grafana database.

relationships.description: Connection target. jsonData.apiType selects how the API base URL is built from environmentId (and domain): 'saas' -> https://<environmentId>.live.dynatrace.com/api/...; 'managed' -> https://<domain>/e/<environmentId>/api/... (domain required); 'url' -> environmentId is the full base URL, with /api/... appended (pkg/dynatrace/client/rest.go:24-53).

Dynatrace tokens. At least one is required (pkg/dynatrace/handler_healthcheck.go:151-153). apiToken authenticates the classic API endpoints as 'Api-Token <token>' (Metrics, Problems, Logs, USQL, audit logs); platformToken authenticates the Grail platform API as 'Bearer <token>' (pkg/dynatrace/client/rest.go:170-179). Set both to use classic and Grail endpoints together.
The CA Cert textarea is rendered only when 'With CA Cert' is enabled (ConfigEditor.tsx:184) and the backend then requires a valid PEM certificate (pkg/models/settings.go:68-76).

instructions.msg: The Dynatrace datasource authenticates with two independent Dynatrace tokens, both stored in secureJsonData and both always shown in the editor (there is no auth-method selector): secureJsonData.apiToken is a classic Dynatrace API token, sent as the 'Authorization: Api-Token <token>' header and used for the classic API endpoints (Metrics, Problems, Logs, USQL, audit logs, etc.); secureJsonData.platformToken is a Dynatrace platform token, sent as 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' and used for the newer Grail platform API. At least one of the two is required; set both to use classic and Grail endpoints together (pkg/dynatrace/client/rest.go:170-179; pkg/dynatrace/handler_healthcheck.go:42-54,151-153).

Minimal payloads. Classic API only: {"jsonData":{"apiType":"saas","environmentId":"<environment-id>"},"secureJsonData":{"apiToken":"<your-dynatrace-api-token>"}}. Grail only: {"jsonData":{"apiType":"saas","environmentId":"<environment-id>"},"secureJsonData":{"platformToken":"<your-dynatrace-platform-token>"}}. If neither token is set, the health check fails with 'Enter an API token or Platform token.' (pkg/dynatrace/handler_healthcheck.go:22,151-153).

secureJsonData values (apiToken, platformToken, tlsCACert) are write-only: they can be set or reset but never read back. To check whether a secret is already configured on an existing datasource, read the secureJsonFields object (e.g. secureJsonFields.apiToken == true) instead of secureJsonData.

jsonData.apiType selects how the API base URL is built and defaults to 'saas' when empty (pkg/dynatrace/client/rest.go:24-53): 'saas' -> https://<environmentId>.live.dynatrace.com/api/... (environmentId is the SaaS environment/tenant ID); 'managed' -> https://<domain>/e/<environmentId>/api/... (jsonData.domain, the Managed cluster host, is also required); 'url' -> environmentId holds the full base URL, e.g. https://myenv.live.dynatrace.com, and /api/... is appended. jsonData.environmentId is always required (pkg/dynatrace/handler_healthcheck.go:142-144). Note the editor relabels the Environment ID field to 'URL' when apiType is 'url'.

For SaaS Grail queries the platform token endpoint switches host from '.live.dynatrace.com' to '.apps.dynatrace.com' and drops the /api/ prefix automatically (pkg/dynatrace/client/rest.go:62-68) — configure only environmentId, not a separate Grail URL. In 'url' mode, environmentId must include the scheme (https://...); the backend concatenates '/api/<version>' onto it.

TLS options live in jsonData: tlsSkipVerify disables server certificate verification; tlsAuthWithCACert=true enables a custom CA and then requires secureJsonData.tlsCACert to be a valid PEM certificate (the backend rejects an empty or unparseable PEM with 'invalid TLS certificate' / 'failed to parse TLS CA PEM certificate', pkg/models/settings.go:64-77). jsonData.httpClientTimeout is the HTTP timeout in seconds and defaults to 30 when unset or <= 0 (pkg/models/settings.go:50-53).
